# Release archival maroons merged PRs: milestone-gated, but PRs carry no milestones

## Context

Operator-reported during the v13.0.0 release thread ("pulls did not get archived at all"), V-B-A'd to mechanism. The v13 archival moved **1,715 issues** into `resources/content/archive/issues/v13.0.0/` but only **120 pulls** into `archive/pulls/v13.0.0/` — while **1,325 merged PRs remain in active `resources/content/pulls/`**, including samples merged squarely in the v12.1.0→v13.0.0 range (`pr-11530.md` closedAt 2026-05-17, `pr-11592.md` 2026-05-18, `pr-11666.md` 2026-05-20).

`Release classification:` post-release data hygiene (the v13 web presence ships either way; the active pulls tree just stays 10× oversized until fixed).

## The Problem

The archival predicate is **milestone-gated**: `resolveMilestoneArchiveBucket` (`ai/services/github-workflow/sync/PullRequestSyncer.mjs:75-92`, mirrored in `IssueSyncer.mjs:86-103`) archives an item only when (a) the target bucket dir was pre-created (the opt-in) AND (b) **the item carries that milestone**. Issues receive milestones in the release flow; PRs overwhelmingly do not → the gate never opens for them. The operator's intended semantics — "a release ARCHIVES all items closed in the timerange; backlog stays" — and the implemented semantics diverge exactly where milestone assignment is sparse.

## The Architectural Reality

- `PullRequestSyncer.mjs:75-92` — the opt-in bucket resolution (existsSync gate + milestone candidate).
- The active pulls corpus: `resources/content/pulls/chunk-*` (1,325 files, mostly `state: MERGED` with in-range dates).
- The analyzer `buildScripts/release/analyzeClosedSinceRelease.mjs:321` already documents the *intended* range semantics: "PR source: `resources/content/pulls/**/*.md` with `state: MERGED` and `mergedAt >= cutoff`" — the range-based definition exists in prose, the syncer implements milestone-based.
- Sibling precision: the issues side worked for v13 because the release flow mass-milestones closed issues; the same sparse-milestone hazard exists there for any unmilestoned closed issue.

## The Fix (shape — implementer verifies against the sync service's invariants)

Either (1) the archival predicate for PRs falls back to **mergedAt-in-release-range** when no milestone is present (range = previous release tag date → current, matching the analyzer's documented semantics), or (2) the release flow mass-assigns the milestone to merged-in-range PRs the way it does for issues, keeping one predicate. Option (2) keeps the syncer untouched and fixes the data at the source of truth (GitHub) — likely the smaller diff and the more truthful record. A one-time backfill (milestone or move) for the 1,325 marooned PRs accompanies either shape.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Merged-in-range PRs archive on the next release cycle without manual milestone curation (whichever predicate shape is chosen).
- [ ] One-time backfill: the currently marooned merged PRs (≈1,325 minus genuinely-post-v13 merges) land in their correct version buckets.
- [ ] The issues/pulls archival predicates end up consistent (same rule or explicitly documented divergence).
- [ ] `analyzeClosedSinceRelease.mjs`'s documented range semantics and the syncer's implemented semantics agree.

## Out of Scope

- The empty-source-folder cleanup gap (sibling ticket, same surface) and the v8-era misattribution residue (separate historical thread, `#12186` family).

## Related

`#12186` (closed syncer-correctness epic), `#12188`/`#12194` (closed bucketing-floor + re-bucket fixes), `#12999` (deep-link map — regenerates over whatever buckets exist, unaffected).

> ● ## Ticket-intake readiness gate — valid premise, but do not branch before #13009 + ledger cleanup
> 
> [ARCH_ALIGNMENT]
> 
> I ran a focused intake pass on #13001 as a post-release cleanup candidate. This is **not a lane claim** and I am not branching.
> 
> Current V-B-A evidence:
> 
> - No open PR currently targets #13001.
> - #13009 / #13002 is now APPROVED + CLEAN, but still OPEN/unmerged; it touches the same syncer family (`IssueSyncer`, `PullRequestSyncer`, `DiscussionSyncer`, plus shared prune helper/tests). Branching #13001 before #13009 lands would create avoidable sync-surface churn.
> - The ticket premise is still valid at the behavior level: `PullRequestSyncer` still derives PR archive placement from milestone/release-date logic, while the release appendix analyzer documents merged-PR range semantics (`resources/content/pulls/**/*.md` with `state: MERGED` and `mergedAt >= cutoff`).
> - The ticket body has a surface-anchor drift: it names `resolveMilestoneArchiveBucket`, but current source names the live helpers `#deriveMilestoneVersion()` and `#deriveClosedAtVersion()` in both `PullRequestSyncer` and `IssueSyncer`. `MetadataManager` also explicitly says PR archive-bucket placement is derived fresh through `PullRequestSyncer#planBuckets`.
> - The ticket modifies a consumed content/archive contract (`resources/content/archive/pulls/<version>/chunk-N/` and active `resources/content/pulls/chunk-N/` behavior), but it does not currently contain a formal Contract Ledger matrix.
> 
> Classification: **needs-contract-alignment + wait for #13009 merge before implementation**.
> 
> Required before branch/code work:
> 
> | Target Surface | Needed contract detail |
> |---|---|
> | `PullRequestSyncer#planBuckets()` / `#deriveMilestoneVersion()` / `#deriveClosedAtVersion()` | Exact precedence between milestone bucket and release-date bucket for merged PRs, including what happens when both exist or neither exists. |
> | `IssueSyncer` parity | Whether issues and pulls keep identical archive-routing rules, or the PR path intentionally diverges because PR milestone coverage differs. |
> | `MetadataManager` PR metadata pruning | Confirm whether `milestone`, `mergedAt`, `closedAt`, and `path` are sufficient for deterministic re-placement without persisting `archiveVersion`. |
> | One-time v13 backfill | Whether the fix is GitHub milestone backfill, local archive relocation, syncer predicate fallback, or a phased combination; include rollback/redo behavior. |
> | Evidence | Corpus count before/after, representative marooned PR samples (`pr-11530`, `pr-11592`, `pr-11666`), unit coverage for the chosen predicate, and a dry-run/backfill proof that active merged PRs move to `archive/pulls/v13.0.0/` without disturbing genuine post-v13 PRs. |
> 
> Once #13009 is merged and this ledger is present, #13001 looks like a valid positive-ROI cleanup lane. Until then, implementation should stay parked to avoid stacking unreviewed archive-sync semantics on top of an open syncer PR.
